# Private Inference for Agents вАФ Live Status

**Hermes wraps every outgoing chat request in an httpx transport that encrypts the conversation to a CVM-attested public key.** Only a TEE-verified GPU running the named model can decrypt it. This page is regenerated by CI every day at 13:17 UTC and on every push to the transport/attestation code, so the results below are always fresh.

[![TEE Attestation](https://github.com/amiller/hermes-agent/actions/workflows/tee-attestation-report.yml/badge.svg?branch=feat/near-ai-attestation)](https://github.com/amiller/hermes-agent/actions/workflows/tee-attestation-report.yml)

## Upstream integration

- **[NousResearch/hermes-agent#12201](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/12201)** вАФ Add NEAR AI + RedPill TDX attestation and the in-process E2EE httpx transport. Branch `feat/near-ai-attestation`.
- Transport lives at [`hermes_cli/e2ee_transport.py`](../hermes_cli/e2ee_transport.py) ¬Ј verifier at [`hermes_cli/attestation.py`](../hermes_cli/attestation.py) ¬Ј script at [`scripts/attestation_report.py`](../scripts/attestation_report.py).

## What this proves

Modern agents leak their full context вАФ memory, credentials, intermediate reasoning, tool outputs вАФ to whichever model they call. This workflow verifies, on every run, that the models Hermes talks to are running inside Intel TDX + NVIDIA confidential-compute enclaves and that the CLI's encryption key is bound to those specific enclaves.

If a row below is вЭМ, the CLI refuses to send plaintext to that model and the `/model` picker hides it. No fallbacks.

## What's verified per row

1. Fetch the provider's live attestation bundle (`GET /v1/attestation/report?model=вА¶&nonce=вА¶`).
2. TDX quote cryptographically verified via Phala's verifier API.
3. `report_data` binds the CVM's signing address and our fresh nonce.
4. NVIDIA GPU attested via NRAS (`/v3/attest/gpu`).
5. `signing_public_key` derives to `signing_address` (keccak of pubkey last 20 bytes).

A вЬЕ means all five held **at this run's timestamp**. The CLI wraps outgoing chat content in an httpx transport that encrypts to that `signing_public_key`, so only the attested model sees the plaintext.
